quick_facts.label: Recruitment channel
quick_facts.value: Junior Clerk Cum Typist is an RRB Non-Technical Popular Categories (NTPC) undergraduate-level post. The RRB Bhubaneswar official notification index lists it under the newer CEN 07/2025 NTPC (Undergraduate) recruitment, while the detailed CEN 06/2024 notice identifies it as an NTPC undergraduate post.

quick_facts.label: Minimum qualification (dated notice context)
quick_facts.value: In CEN 06/2024, the requirement was 12th (+2 stage) or equivalent with at least 50% aggregate marks; the 50% threshold did not apply to SC/ST, PwBD, ex-servicemen, or candidates with qualifications higher than 12th. Computer typing proficiency in English or Hindi was essential. Applicants must use the eligibility terms in the specific current CEN.

quick_facts.label: Selection stages (dated notice context)
quick_facts.value: CEN 06/2024 used first-stage CBT, second-stage CBT, a qualifying typing skill test for this post, document verification, and medical examination. Appointment is offered by the relevant Railway Administration after selection and required checks.

quick_facts.label: Typing benchmark (dated notice context)
quick_facts.value: CEN 06/2024 specified 30 words per minute in English or 25 words per minute in Hindi on a personal computer, without editing tools or spell-check facilities. The typing test was qualifying; its marks were not added to merit.

quick_facts.label: Pay level and medical category (dated notice context)
quick_facts.value: CEN 06/2024 placed Junior Clerk Cum Typist at 7th CPC Pay Level 2, with an initial basic pay of ₹19,900 and medical standard C2. Basic pay, allowances, vacancies, age limits, and medical provisions can change by notification and applicable rules.

sections.heading: Entry through RRB NTPC undergraduate recruitment
paragraphs: The normal external entry route is an RRB NTPC undergraduate Centralised Employment Notice (CEN). Candidates apply online to one chosen RRB for vacancies notified for that recruitment cycle, rank the posts and Railway/Production Unit preferences for which they are eligible, and proceed through the prescribed selection stages.

The detailed CEN 06/2024 route illustrates the process: first-stage CBT is used to shortlist for second-stage CBT; for Junior Clerk Cum Typist, second-stage CBT performance is used to shortlist candidates for the typing skill test. Candidates then undergo document verification and a Railway medical fitness examination. RRBs recommend empanelled candidates, but the respective Railway Administration makes the appointment offer.

Because post parameters, age cut-offs, vacancies, fee arrangements, dates, and relaxation provisions are notification-specific, candidates should treat an active RRB CEN—not older notices or coaching sites—as the controlling document. The RRB Bhubaneswar index shows Junior Clerk Cum Typist in CEN 07/2025 NTPC (Undergraduate), demonstrating that recruitment is issued in cycles.
Check the relevant live RRB notification and apply only through an official RRB portal.
Hold the required 12th/equivalent qualification and computer typing capability specified in that CEN.
Prepare for two CBT stages, then the qualifying typing test if shortlisted.
Complete document verification and the Railway Administration's medical examination if selected.
bullets: Check the relevant live RRB notification and apply only through an official RRB portal.
Hold the required 12th/equivalent qualification and computer typing capability specified in that CEN.
Prepare for two CBT stages, then the qualifying typing test if shortlisted.
Complete document verification and the Railway Administration's medical examination if selected.

sections.heading: Skills demonstrated in recruitment
paragraphs: The clearest role-specific skill formally tested for this post is computer typing. Under CEN 06/2024, candidates for Junior Clerk Cum Typist had to meet a qualifying benchmark of 30 WPM in English or 25 WPM in Hindi on a personal computer without editing tools or spell-check. Hindi test-takers could use Kruti Dev or Mangal fonts.

The CBT syllabus in the same notice covered mathematics, general intelligence and reasoning, general awareness, and basics of computers and computer applications. This makes accurate keyboarding, basic computer familiarity, numerical comfort, reasoning, and careful reading practical preparation priorities. These are examination requirements rather than a complete employer-issued description of daily duties.
bullets: Computer typing in English or Hindi.
Mathematics, reasoning, and general-awareness preparation for CBTs.

sections.heading: Employer and posting context
paragraphs: This is a Ministry of Railways/Indian Railways clerical recruitment category. In CEN 06/2024, RRBs invited applications for NTPC undergraduate posts in various Zonal Railways and Production Units, and the vacancy annexures allocated Junior Clerk Cum Typist positions across named railways and units. A candidate's final organisation and location therefore depend on the vacancies, preferences, merit, medical fitness, and allotment in the applicable recruitment cycle.

The post is listed with medical standard C2 in the 2024 detailed notice. Candidates should not assume a particular zone, production unit, city, or vacancy count from a previous CEN; these elements vary by cycle.
bullets: Employment is within the Indian Railways system, through the relevant Railway Administration.
Posting opportunities may arise in Zonal Railways and Production Units.
Organisation and location are allotment-dependent rather than guaranteed by the title alone.

sections.heading: Pay context
paragraphs: For context only, CEN 06/2024 specified 7th CPC Pay Level 2 and initial basic pay of ₹19,900 for Junior Clerk Cum Typist. This is basic pay, not a universal take-home salary figure: actual monthly pay can be affected by applicable allowances, deductions, posting location, and later government or Railway pay orders.

The 2024 figure should be verified against the active CEN and current Railway/Central Government pay instructions before making a financial decision. It is included here as a dated recruitment-notice reference, not as a promise of current net compensation.
bullets: Dated CEN 06/2024 pay matrix position: 7th CPC Level 2.
Take-home pay is variable and should be checked using current official orders and the active recruitment notice.
